sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Elizabeth is a 53-year-old female complaining of chest pain that started approximately three months ago.
The pain is described as a dull, pressure-like sensation that occurs mainly when she walks and has not been relieved by rest.
She denies any chest pain at the moment.
She has a past medical history of diabetes and high blood pressure, although she was unaware of her high blood pressure until today.
She is currently on metformin 500mg twice a day for her diabetes.
Past medical history: Elizabeth has a history of diabetes and is currently on metformin 500mg twice a day.
She also has a family history of heart disease.
She is a smoker, but the amount is not specified.
Review of systems: Elizabeth denies any shortness of breath, sweats, clamminess, or nausea.
Current medications: Metformin 500mg twice a day.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The physical exam was normal except for a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, and 1+ nonpitting edema in the lower legs.
Test Results: EKG performed today was normal, showing no signs of a heart attack.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is a 53-year-old female with suspected unstable angina, diabetes, and high blood pressure.
Problem list: Unstable angina; diabetes; high blood pressure; suspected heart disease.
Plan:
I prescribed aspirin 81mg once a day for chest pain management.
I also prescribed nitroglycerin for the patient to take if the chest pain does not resolve after 1-2 pills.
I referred the patient to cardiology for further evaluation and stress testing.
I instructed the patient to return in two weeks for follow-up.
I also instructed the patient to use a blood pressure cuff at home and enter the readings in EPIC.
Follow-up: The patient is to return in two weeks for follow-up and to have a stress test.
